_note: Waste-pattern catalog = the coach's rules, kept separate from analyzer logic. Each pattern names a metric computed by analyze.py, a comparison, and the coaching message (placeholders in {braces} are filled from the metrics). This coach is token-first - figures are token counts and turns, not dollars. Tune thresholds, edit copy, or add patterns freely.

patterns.title: Low prompt-cache hit rate
patterns.message: Only {cache_hit_pct}% of input context was served from cache. Re-sending uncached context every turn is the #1 token waste. Keep stable context (system prompt, instructions, pinned files) at the FRONT and unchanged so it stays cached; avoid reordering or editing early context mid-session. A cache hit costs about a tenth of a fresh input token, so keeping context stable is the single biggest token saver.

patterns.title: Context bloat
patterns.message: Peak context reached {peak_context_tokens:,} tokens (avg {avg_context_tokens:,}). Every token in context is re-billed on every later turn, so a heavy context taxes the rest of the session. Compact the conversation, drop stale tool output, or start a fresh thread for each new sub-task.

patterns.title: Oversized tool output in context
patterns.message: A single tool result added ~{largest_tool_output_tokens:,} tokens to context (e.g. a whole-file read or an unbounded command dump). That payload is then re-billed on every following turn. Read targeted line ranges instead of whole files, grep for the lines you need, and pipe noisy commands through head/tail or a filter before they land in context.

patterns.title: Failed tool calls
patterns.message: {tool_error_count} tool call(s) returned an error ({tool_error_pct}% of all calls). Each failure still costs a full round-trip — the call, the error, and the retry are all billed, and the error text stays in context. Check paths and command syntax before running, and prefer a quick dry-run over trial-and-error on expensive steps.

patterns.title: Output-heavy spend
patterns.message: Output tokens are {output_share_pct}% of cost, and output is the priciest token class (~5x the input rate). Ask for terser answers, smaller diffs, and 'no preamble' to trim it.

patterns.title: Everything on the priciest model
patterns.message: {pct_on_priciest}% of spend ran on {priciest_model}. Route mechanical steps (renames, greps, boilerplate, file reads) to a cheaper tier and reserve the top model for real reasoning.

patterns.title: Redundant file reads
patterns.message: Re-read the same file(s) {redundant_read_count} extra time(s); most re-read: {top_reread_file}. Each re-read re-injects the file into context and re-bills it. Read once and keep it, or read targeted line ranges.

patterns.title: Very long single thread
patterns.message: {n_assistant_msgs} assistant turns in one thread. A long thread carries its whole history forward at full cost every turn. Split unrelated work into fresh sessions to shed dead context.
